title: AI Has Ruined the Job Market
description: Maybe flawed people were better than brute algorithms.
author: Annie Lowrey

title: The Ordinary Miracle of Existing
description: Being alive at all is the most extraordinary stroke of good luck any of us will ever experience.
author: Alan Lightman

title: Trump’s Strange Choice for Director of National Intelligence
description: The president’s selection of Bill Pulte is both baffling and predictable.
author: David Frum

title: Where Dogs Go On With Their Doggy Life
description: Why are there so many canines in fine art?
author: Judith Shulevitz

title: The Art of the Joyful Tearjerker
description: A new novel by theHamnetauthor Maggie O’Farrell showcases her genius for infusing painful stories with flashes of pure bliss.
author: Hillary Kelly

title: The Strange Appeal of the Solitude Influencer
description: Some of them say they have no friends—and they love it.
author: Faith Hill

title: There Is Already a Word for the Deep Moral Failures of AI
author: Tyler Austin Harper

title: Democrats Must Learn to Talk Sports
description: Politicians need as much attention as possible, as frequently as possible, while seeming as relatable as possible. A cheat code exists to hit all three objectives.
author: Nathaniel Frum

title: How Much of Data-Center Activism Is Really AI Slop?
description: Anti-AI sentiment is genuine, but its online expression looks stranger and stranger.
author: Kaitlyn Tiffany

title: How Raphael Made It All Look So Easy
description: A Met exhibition devoted to the Renaissance painter shows the artist letting loose.
author: Susan Tallman

title: AtlanticTrivia: Opera and the Atmosphere
description: Test your knowledge—and read our stories for a little extra help.
author: Drew Goins

title: All These Defeats Are Ruining Trump’s Birthday
description: The end of Trump’s “anti-weaponization” fund is the latest in a series of setbacks.
author: Idrees Kahloon

title: Why Did Donald Trump Get So Suddenly Shy?
description: As his signature efforts falter, the president is pleading with his critics to pipe down and pay less attention.
author: David A. Graham

title: That’s Enough,Euphoria
description: In its final episode, the series gave in to its worst impulses while trying to sell a poignant message.
author: Shirley Li

title: Photos: Farming in Ukraine’s War Zone
description: After more than four years of warfare, Ukrainian farmers face severe obstacles as they try to continue working their fields. Hazards and challenges include Russian drone attacks, mined fields, damaged
author: Alan Taylor

title: The White House Is the New Green Zone
description: Fortifications are growing in tandem with the threat of political violence.
author: Matt Viser

title: Hope, Change, Troll
description: Spencer Pratt, the reality star people love to hate-watch, is running for office—and betting that infamy can be political currency.
author: Louis Staples

title: How to Silence the Federal Workforce
description: A proposal to push would-be whistleblowers to sign nondisclosure agreements is part of a bigger effort to hide government secrets.
author: Quinta Jurecic

title: How Iran Killed Its Economy
description: For a moment, Iran had a thriving tech industry that promised people a better future. Then the regime mounted a terrifying backlash.
author: Yeganeh TorbatiandBozorgmehr Sharafedin

title: The Spanish Exception
description: The country’s leaders avoided a populist backlash by engineering an economic boom. Now the boom is creating problems of its own.
author: Rogé Karma
